title: Player Input & Building Placement

shared_InputHandler: block: Block — currently selected block type (null = none)
isBuilding: boolean — whether auto-building is active
selectPlans: Seq<BuildPlan> — plans queued for placement (mobile staging / schematic)
linePlans: Seq<BuildPlan> — plans in active line/drag selection
playerPlanTree: QuadTree<BuildPlan> — spatial index for overlap checking
DesktopInput_only: selectX/Y: int — drag start tile coordinates
splan: BuildPlan — plan currently being repositioned
deleting: boolean — actively removing queued break plans
MobileInput_only: lineMode: boolean — long-press line drag active

desktop_placement_flow: Check player.dead() or locked() → if true, run pollInputNoPlayer() and STOP building logic
User selects block from UI → sets InputHandler.block, resets mode
keyTap(Binding.select) && isPlacing() [block != null] → set selectX/Y = cursorX/Y, mode = placing, call updateLine(selectX, selectY)
While key held: if cursor moved → updateLine(selectX, selectY) rebuilds linePlans via iterateLine()
updateLine(): clears linePlans, iterates line geometry, creates BuildPlan(x,y,rotation,block,block.nextConfig()) per tile
If settings.blockreplace: each plan checks block.getReplacement() to substitute block type
keyRelease(Binding.select) while mode == placing && block != null:
flushPlans(): for each plan → validPlace(x,y,block,rotation) → if valid: plan.copy(), block.onNewPlan(copy), player.unit().addBuild(copy), playerPlanTree.insert(copy)
removeSelection(): normalizeArea(), for each tile → tryBreakBlock(x,y)
keyTap(Binding.deselect) while isPlacing() → block = null, mode = none
mobile_placement_flow: START: update() called each frame — manages mode transitions
Mode sync rules in update(): block!=null && mode==none → mode=placing; block==null && mode==placing → mode=none
User taps block from UI → sets InputHandler.block → update() sets mode=placing
Get cursor tile; Call.tileTap(player, cursor); checkTargets(worldx, worldy)
selectPlans.add(lastPlaced = new BuildPlan(cursor.x, cursor.y, rotation, block, block.nextConfig()))
longPress(x, y): if mode==placing || mode==breaking → lineMode=true, set lineStartX/Y
If block != null: updateLine(lineStartX, lineStartY, cursor.x, cursor.y)
update(): if lineMode && cursor moved → updateLine(lineStartX, lineStartY, lx, ly)
autoPan() called when near screen edges during line/schematic select
touchUp(): if lineMode && mode==placing → flushSelectPlans(linePlans) → adds valid plans to selectPlans; Events.fire(LineConfirmEvent)
touchUp(): if lineMode && mode==breaking → removeSelection(lineStartX,lineStartY,tileX,tileY,true) → adds break plans to selectPlans
For each breaking plan: tryBreakBlock(tile.x, tile.y)
touchDown() with schematicMode: mode = rebuildMode ? rebuildSelect : schematicSelect; set lineStartX/Y
touchUp() with mode==schematicSelect: schematics.create() → useSchematic() → selectPlans populated
Schematic UI buttons: flipPlans(selectPlans, true/false), rotatePlans(selectPlans, 1)
Cancel button: player.unit().clearBuilding(), selectPlans.clear(), mode=none, block=null
Break button toggle: mode = (mode==breaking ? block==null ? none : placing : breaking)
shared_validation_flow: Build.validPlace(type, player.team(), x, y, rotation) [static check: bounds, tile solid, team rules]
IF player.isBuilder(): intersect playerPlanTree for overlapping queued plans
Returns false if overlapping non-replaceable plan exists
checkOverlapPlacement(x, y, block) [Mobile] — checks both selectPlans and player.unit().plans() for overlap
key_conditions_and_branches: scene.hasMouse() → skip world input if UI has focus
isBuilding flag → auto-pause building queue; toggled by pauseBuilding binding
commandMode → overrides placement input with unit/building command mode
settings.blockreplace → substitutes block type with replacement in line plans
settings.doubletapmine → requires double-tap to begin mining
Binding.boost (Desktop) → reverses flush order (flushPlansReverse vs flushPlans)
key_method_signatures: InputHandler.validPlace(int x, int y, Block type, int rotation, BuildPlan ignore, boolean ignoreUnits): boolean
InputHandler.updateLine(int x1, int y1, int x2, int y2): void
InputHandler.removeSelection(int x1, int y1, int x2, int y2, boolean flush, int maxLength): void
InputHandler.useSchematic(Schematic schem, boolean checkHidden): void [overridden by both subclasses]
InputHandler.rebuildArea(int x1, int y1, int x2, int y2): void
MobileInput.tap(float x, float y, int count, KeyCode button): boolean
MobileInput.touchDown(int screenX, int screenY, int pointer, KeyCode button): boolean
MobileInput.touchUp(int screenX, int screenY, int pointer, KeyCode button): boolean
MobileInput.pan(float x, float y, float deltaX, float deltaY): boolean
important_notes: Desktop uses keyboard/mouse drag to define line of blocks; confirmed on key release
Mobile uses single tap to queue individual blocks into selectPlans; long press activates line mode; confirm button flushes all
Both paths converge at player.unit().addBuild(BuildPlan) — the unit's build queue
Build.validPlace / Build.validBreak are static world-state validators (bounds, tile occupancy, team)
playerPlanTree (QuadTree) is used for fast overlap detection against already-queued plans
Schematic placement differs: Desktop places immediately via flushPlans; Mobile populates selectPlans for manual confirm
MobileInput.pan() enables dragging existing selectPlans to new positions (selecting mode)
Break mode (Desktop) produces break BuildPlans via removeSelection→tryBreakBlock→breakBlock; (Mobile) adds them to selectPlans for confirm-button approval
